name: Autophagy Induction Coverage Repair Assessment for June 11, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Coverage repair created after the June 11 surveillance assessment flagged active/no-results registry normalization, review triangulation, and taxonomy-boundary cleanup before ordinary surveillance resumed.

summary: Coverage is now adequate for the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal public claim, pending review and publication of the staged repair bundle. The repair stages direct completed or active no-results registry context for KH-1, spermidine, fasting-mimicking diet, and trehalose, plus a direct 2026 polyamine/autophagy review boundary. It does not make the evidence strong because no newly staged source reports durable human functional, disease, frailty, mortality, or broad healthy-aging benefit attributable to macroautophagic flux.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public map already contains spermidine, FMD, and vaccine-response result-bearing human studies; the repair stages registry context for KH-1, active spermidine studies, FMD immunosenescence, and trehalose autophagic flux.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The remaining issue is an evidence gap: no durable older-human functional or disease/mortality-relevant benefit has been shown.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair adds direct mechanistic watch context for KH-1 autophagy endpoints, spermidine polyamine/eIF5A and muscle autophagy context, trehalose autophagic-flux protein expression, and a 2026 polyamine/autophagy muscle-aging review.

The repair keeps null longer-duration spermidine evidence visible and adds completed/no-results, stale/no-results, and disease-context no-results records as limiting context rather than evidence progress.

Existing spermidine tolerability records and staged registry safety endpoints cover short-term and pending safety context, but long-term safety, durability, and broad older-adult risk-benefit remain thin.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Upgrade only after longer and larger posted safety data appear for older, frail, or disease-context populations.

evidence_categories.rationale: The high-priority registry landscape is repaired by staging NCT06445569, NCT07383311, NCT06186102, NCT04928963, and NCT05593549 while explicitly excluding NCT07255300 and NCT07354620 as adjacent context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Continue ordinary surveillance for status changes, registry results, linked publications, and long-stale no-results records.

evidence_categories.rationale: PMID 42086115 is staged as the direct 2026 polyamine/autophagy muscle-aging review. PMID 42169620 and PMID 42225652 were checked and excluded as vaccine/mTOR/microbiota context and broad aging-therapeutics context, respectively.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Review coverage is adequate for taxonomy and mechanism, but not a substitute for posted trial results.

evidence_categories.rationale: The prior result-bearing spermidine and FMD anchors remain represented, and the repair does not displace them.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Seminal-source coverage is adequate for current claims, not strong enough for a durable human-benefit claim.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair separates direct autophagy/spermidine/polyamine and autophagic-flux context from adjacent FMD, immune-response, mTOR, rapamycin, metabolic, exercise, and broad aging-review context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future publication should keep NCT07255300 and NCT07354620 in adjacent-track surveillance unless posted results cleanly isolate autophagy endpoints.

search_log_summary: Coverage repair directly checked the flagged ClinicalTrials.gov records through API v2, fetched PubMed metadata and abstracts for the three flagged 2026 reviews, staged the direct registry and review records that materially improve public source coverage, and explicitly excluded adjacent FMD follow-up, rapamycin/ProLon, vaccine/mTOR, and broad aging-therapeutics review context.
source_selection_notes: NCT06445569 was staged because it is a completed no-results KH-1 trial whose title and endpoints directly name autophagy.
NCT07383311 and NCT06186102 were staged because they are active spermidine/polyamine registries with autophagy, polyamine, functional, cognition, sleep, muscle, and cardiometabolic endpoints.
NCT04928963 was staged because it is stale unknown-status FMD immunosenescence/frailty registry context that should be visible as a boundary and watch item.
NCT05593549 was staged because it directly measures autophagic-flux protein expression after trehalose, while being clearly marked as short mechanistic disease-context evidence.
PMID 42086115 was staged as direct polyamine/autophagy review context; PMID 42169620 and PMID 42225652 were excluded as adjacent review context.
NCT07255300 and NCT07354620 were excluded from public staging to avoid collapsing single-arm FMD follow-up and rapamycin/ProLon methylation-age combination context into Autophagy Induction.

known_gaps.description: No staged source shows durable broad healthy-aging, frailty, disease, mortality, or repeated older-human functional benefit attributable to macroautophagic flux.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Continue surveillance and only revisit stage if posted trial results pair credible autophagy target engagement with meaningful aging-relevant outcomes.

known_gaps.description: Spermidine, FMD, KH-1, and trehalose safety and durability evidence remains thin in older, frail, and disease-context populations.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Track posted registry results, adverse-event data, and longer follow-up publications.

next_coverage_action: Review the staged coverage-repair bundle. If approved and published, return Autophagy Induction to ordinary surveillance with no immediate additional coverage-repair blocker.
